Finish Templ migration for all core pages Problem: Core pages are still rendered with html/template handlers instead of Templ components. Evidence: - repo/cmd/server/main.go:74-78 (ParseGlob + SetHTMLTemplate) - repo/internal/handlers/booking_handlers.go:55 - repo/internal/handlers/menu_handlers.go:80 - repo/internal/handlers/health_handlers.go:390 - repo/internal/views/dashboard.templ:164 (Templ is already used for dashboard) How to fix: - Create new Templ pages for booking/menu/clinician/admin in repo/internal/views: - bookings.templ - menu.templ - clinician.templ - admin_users.templ - admin_performance.templ - admin_webhooks.templ - In handlers, replace c.HTML(...) responses with views.Render(...): - repo/internal/handlers/booking_handlers.go - repo/internal/handlers/menu_handlers.go - repo/internal/handlers/health_handlers.go (ClinicianPage) - repo/internal/handlers/auth_handlers.go (UsersPage/RegisterPage/Register errors) - repo/internal/handlers/admin_handlers.go - After full migration, remove or reduce html/template bootstrapping in repo/cmd/server/main.go. 2. Add explicit on-prem SSO sync (separate from CSV enrollment) Problem: Only watched-folder CSV import is implemented; there is no dedicated SSO import adapter/scheduler. Evidence: - repo/internal/services/integration.go:58 (processFiles) - repo/internal/services/integration.go:119 (importEnrollment) - repo/internal/services/integration.go:198 (importOrgStructure) - repo/cmd/server/main.go:50 (NewCSVWatcher) How to fix: - Add a dedicated SSO sync service: - new file repo/internal/services/sso_sync.go - define interface for internal SSO source, fetch users/groups, map to User/DepartmentRecord - Add scheduler startup in repo/cmd/server/main.go: - start ticker goroutine (for example every 15 minutes) - call ssoSync.RunOnce() - Extend configuration in repo/internal/config/config.go: - SSO sync interval - SSO source path/DSN/token for internal network source - Add audit logging in sync path using repo/internal/services/audit.go for user/role/department changes. 3. Enforce audit immutability at DB level Problem: Audit data is appended via code, but DB-level UPDATE/DELETE protections are not defined for audit tables. Evidence: - repo/internal/services/audit.go:21 (LogChange insert path) - repo/internal/models/database.go:62 (AuditLog model migration) - repo/internal/models/models.go:149-157 (AuditLog schema) How to fix: - In repo/internal/models/database.go, add SQL after AutoMigrate to block modifications: - create function prevent_audit_modifications() - create trigger on audit_logs before update or delete -> raise exception - Apply same pattern for booking_audits if required: - table booking_audits in repo/internal/models/models.go - Ensure app DB role used by service has no delete/update grants on audit tables (migration SQL in same file or deployment SQL script). 4. Formalize named integrations (competition platform, data warehouse) Problem: Webhook plumbing exists, but named systems from requirements are not represented as explicit integration contracts/workflows. Evidence: - repo/internal/services/integration.go:272 (RegisterEndpointForOrg) - repo/internal/services/integration.go:288 (DispatchForOrg) - repo/cmd/server/main.go:197 (internal webhook receiver) - repo/internal/templates/admin_webhooks.html:24-25 (only e-signature/plagiarism options shown) How to fix: - Add explicit event type constants and payload schemas: - new file repo/internal/services/integration_contracts.go - include competition.* and warehouse.* events - Add typed dispatcher helpers (instead of raw string event types): - repo/internal/services/integration.go - methods like DispatchCompetitionResult(...) and DispatchWarehouseExportReady(...) - Update admin integration UI options: - repo/internal/templates/admin_webhooks.html - add event options for competition and warehouse - Add internal receiver validation/mapping for these contracts: - repo/cmd/server/main.go (or move receiver handler into repo/internal/handlers/admin_handlers.go)

22		data, err := json.Marshal(snapshot)
23		if err != nil {
24			return err
25		}

28	
29		entry := &models.AuditLog{
30			TableName:   tableName,

32			Action:      action,
33			EditorID:    editorID,
34			Reason:      reason,
35			Fingerprint: fingerprint,
36			Snapshot:    string(data),
37			Timestamp:   time.Now(),
38		}
39	
40		return s.DB.Create(entry).Error
41	}

16	func InitDB(cfg *config.Config) *gorm.DB {
17		dsn := fmt.Sprintf(
18			"host=%s port=%s user=%s password=%s dbname=%s sslmode=%s",
19			cfg.DBHost, cfg.DBPort, cfg.DBUser, cfg.DBPassword, cfg.DBName, cfg.DBSSLMode,
20		)
21	
22		var db *gorm.DB
23		var err error
24	
25		// Retry loop — wait for Postgres to become ready (critical for Docker Compose)
26		for i := 0; i < 30; i++ {
27			db, err = gorm.Open(postgres.Open(dsn), &gorm.Config{
28				Logger: logger.Default.LogMode(logger.Warn),
29			})
30			if err == nil {
31				sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
32				if pingErr := sqlDB.Ping(); pingErr == nil {
33					break
34				}
35			}
36			log.Printf("Waiting for database to be ready... (%d/30)", i+1)
37			time.Sleep(2 * time.Second)
38		}
39		if err != nil {
40			log.Fatalf("Failed to connect to database after retries: %v", err)
41		}
42	
43		sqlDB, _ := db.DB()
44		sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(25)
45		sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(10)
46		sqlDB.SetConnMaxLifetime(5 * time.Minute)
47	
48		return db
49	}

51	func AutoMigrate(db *gorm.DB) {
52		err := db.AutoMigrate(
53			&Organization{},
54			&DepartmentRecord{},
55			&User{},
56			&TempAccess{},
57			&Session{},
58			&HealthRecord{},
59			&Vital{},
60			&Encounter{},
61			&Attachment{},
62			&AuditLog{},
63			&Venue{},
64			&TrainerProfile{},
65			&Booking{},
66			&BookingAudit{},
67			&MenuCategory{},
68			&MenuItem{},
69			&MenuItemChoice{},
70			&ItemSubstitute{},
71			&SellWindow{},
72			&HolidayBlackout{},
73			&Promotion{},
74			&MenuOrder{},
75			&MenuOrderItem{},
76			&APIToken{},
77			&WebhookEndpoint{},
78			&WebhookDelivery{},
79			&ReportJob{},
80			&SlowQueryLog{},
81		)
82		if err != nil {
83			log.Fatalf("AutoMigrate failed: %v", err)
84		}
85	
86		// Create vitals partitioning by month.
87		// GORM creates a regular vitals table; we convert it to partitioned if it isn't already.
88		db.Exec(`
89			DO $$
90			DECLARE
91				month_start DATE;
92				month_end DATE;
93				part_name TEXT;
94				is_partitioned BOOLEAN;
95			BEGIN
96				-- Check if the vitals table is already partitioned
97				SELECT EXISTS(
98					SELECT 1 FROM pg_partitioned_table
99					WHERE partrelid = 'vitals'::regclass
100				) INTO is_partitioned;
101	
102				IF NOT is_partitioned THEN
103					-- Backup, drop, and recreate as partitioned
104					CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vitals_backup AS SELECT * FROM vitals;
105					DROP TABLE IF EXISTS vitals CASCADE;
106					CREATE TABLE vitals (
107						id SERIAL,
108						user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
109						weight_lb NUMERIC(6,1),
110						bp_systolic INTEGER,
111						bp_diastolic INTEGER,
112						temperature_f NUMERIC(5,1),
113						heart_rate INTEGER,
114						recorded_by INTEGER NOT NULL,
115						recorded_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL,
116						created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
117						PRIMARY KEY (id, recorded_at)
118					) PARTITION BY RANGE (recorded_at);
119	
120					-- Restore data if any existed
121					INSERT INTO vitals (user_id, weight_lb, bp_systolic, bp_diastolic,
122						temperature_f, heart_rate, recorded_by, recorded_at, created_at)
123					SELECT user_id, weight_lb, bp_systolic, bp_diastolic,
124						temperature_f, heart_rate, recorded_by, recorded_at, created_at
125					FROM vitals_backup
126					WHERE recorded_at IS NOT NULL;
127					DROP TABLE vitals_backup;
128				END IF;
129	
130				-- Rolling partition: create partitions from 2025 through 2 years in the future
131				FOR yr IN 2025..(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM NOW())::INT + 2) LOOP
132					FOR m IN 1..12 LOOP
133						month_start := make_date(yr, m, 1);
134						month_end := month_start + '1 month'::INTERVAL;
135						part_name := 'vitals_' || TO_CHAR(month_start, 'YYYY_MM');
136	
137						IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = part_name) THEN
138							EXECUTE format(
139								'CREATE TABLE %I PARTITION OF vitals FOR VALUES FROM (%L) TO (%L)',
140								part_name, month_start, month_end
141							);
142						END IF;
143					END LOOP;
144				END LOOP;
145	
146				-- Create indexes
147				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'idx_vitals_recorded_at') THEN
148					CREATE INDEX idx_vitals_recorded_at ON vitals (recorded_at);
149				END IF;
150				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = 'idx_vitals_user_id') THEN
151					CREATE INDEX idx_vitals_user_id ON vitals (user_id);
152				END IF;
153			END $$;
154		`)
155	
156		// Create org-scoped materialized views for reporting.
157		// Drop and recreate to ensure organization_id column is present (migration from
158		// earlier schema that lacked the org dimension).
159		db.Exec(`DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW IF EXISTS mv_clinic_utilization`)
160		db.Exec(`DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW IF EXISTS mv_booking_fill_rates`)
161		db.Exec(`DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW IF EXISTS mv_menu_sell_through`)
162	
163		db.Exec(`
164			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv_clinic_utilization AS
165			SELECT
166				date_trunc('day', e.encounter_date) AS day,
167				e.department,
168				u.organization_id,
169				COUNT(*) AS encounter_count
170			FROM encounters e
171			JOIN users u ON u.id = e.clinician_id
172			GROUP BY day, e.department, u.organization_id
173			ORDER BY day DESC;
174		`)
175	
176		db.Exec(`
177			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv_booking_fill_rates AS
178			SELECT
179				date_trunc('day', b.slot_start) AS day,
180				b.venue_id,
181				b.organization_id,
182				COUNT(*) AS total_bookings,
183				COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE b.status = 'confirmed') AS confirmed,
184				COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE b.status = 'canceled') AS canceled
185			FROM bookings b
186			GROUP BY day, b.venue_id, b.organization_id
187			ORDER BY day DESC;
188		`)
189	
190		db.Exec(`
191			CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv_menu_sell_through AS
192			SELECT
193				mi.sku,
194				mi.name,
195				mi.organization_id,
196				COALESCE(SUM(moi.quantity), 0) AS total_sold,
197				COALESCE(SUM(moi.quantity * moi.unit_price), 0) AS total_revenue
198			FROM menu_items mi
199			LEFT JOIN menu_order_items moi ON moi.menu_item_id = mi.id
200			GROUP BY mi.id, mi.sku, mi.name, mi.organization_id
201			ORDER BY total_sold DESC;
202		`)
203	
204		// Create unique indexes on materialized views (required for REFRESH CONCURRENTLY)
205		db.Exec(`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS mv_clinic_util_idx ON mv_clinic_utilization (day, department, organization_id)`)
206		db.Exec(`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS mv_booking_fill_idx ON mv_booking_fill_rates (day, venue_id, organization_id)`)
207		db.Exec(`CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS mv_menu_sell_idx ON mv_menu_sell_through (sku, organization_id)`)
208	
209		// Create audit log trigger function
210		db.Exec(`
211			CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION create_unique_index_if_not_exists(idx_name text, tbl text, col text)
212			RETURNS void AS $$
213			BEGIN
214				IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_class WHERE relname = idx_name) THEN
215					EXECUTE format('CREATE UNIQUE INDEX %I ON %I (%s)', idx_name, tbl, col);
216				END IF;
217			END $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
218		`)
219	
220		log.Println("Database migration completed successfully")
221	}

37	func Load() *Config {
38		return &Config{
39			DBHost:     getEnv("DB_HOST", "localhost"),
40			DBPort:     getEnv("DB_PORT", "5432"),
41			DBUser:     getEnv("DB_USER", "campus_admin"),
42			DBPassword: getEnv("DB_PASSWORD", "campus_secret"),

46			ServerPort: getEnv("SERVER_PORT", "8080"),
47			SessionKey: requireEnvOrGenerate("SESSION_KEY"),
48			HMACSecret: requireEnvOrGenerate("HMAC_SECRET"),
49	
50			UploadDir:    getEnv("UPLOAD_DIR", "./uploads"),
51			WatchedDir:   getEnv("WATCHED_DIR", "./watched_folder"),
52			MaxUploadMB:  10,
53			AllowedTypes: []string{"application/pdf", "image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/gif"},
54	
55			RateLimitPerMin:   60,
56			TempAccessDur:     8 * time.Hour,
57			SlowQueryMs:       500,
58			CacheTTL:          5 * time.Minute,
59			MVRefreshInterval: 15 * time.Minute,
60			// Default secure cookies to true in release mode, false in dev
61			SecureCookies: func() bool {
62				explicit := os.Getenv("SECURE_COOKIES")
63				if explicit != "" {
64					return explicit == "true"
65				}
66				return os.Getenv("GIN_MODE") == "release"
67			}(),
68		}
69	}
70	
71	func getEnv(key, fallback string) string {
72		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
73			return v
74		}
75		return fallback
76	}
77	
78	// requireEnvOrGenerate returns the env var value, or generates a random secret
79	// and warns at startup. Production deployments MUST set these via env vars.
80	func requireEnvOrGenerate(key string) string {
81		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
82			return v
83		}
84		b := make([]byte, 32)
85		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
86			log.Fatalf("Failed to generate random secret for %s: %v", key, err)
87		}
88		generated := hex.EncodeToString(b)
89		log.Printf("WARNING: %s not set — generated random value. Set this env var for production.", key)
90		return generated
91	}

### **Key Architecture Patterns**

1. **Multi-Tenancy**: organization_id on almost all tables, enforced in handlers
2. **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)**: 5 roles (student, faculty, clinician, staff, admin)
3. **Data Scoping**:
   - Students/Faculty: only their own health/booking records
   - Clinicians: their department's patients
   - Staff: specific departments (based on DepartmentID)
   - Admin: entire organization
4. **Security**:
   - CSRF tokens on form submissions
   - File type validation (magic bytes + declared type)
   - HMAC signed API requests
   - Rate limiting (60/min)
   - Session-based auth with secure cookies
   - Locally-issued API tokens with expiration
5. **Audit Trail**: all changes logged with fingerprinting for compliance
6. **CSV Integration**: automatic user/department sync from CSV files
7. **Webhook System**: internal-only URLs, signed payloads, 3 retries
8. **Reporting**: materialized views refreshed every 15 minutes, cached for 5 minutes
9. **Database Optimization**:
   - Vitals table partitioned by month
   - Connection pooling (25 open, 10 idle)
   - Slow query logging (>500ms)

### **Views (Templ Components)**

**internal/views/layout.templ** (96 lines):
- `Layout()` - Base HTML layout with navbar
  - Conditional nav links based on user role (clinician, staff, admin)
  - Renders contents as templ.Component
- `LoginPage()` - Login form with CSRF protection
- `ErrorPage()` - Error display template

**internal/views/dashboard.templ** (167 lines):
- Structures: VitalRow, EncounterRow, AttachmentRow, DashboardData, UserInfo
- `DashboardContent()` - Health dashboard content

  - Recent encounters table
- `DashboardPage()` - Wraps content in Layout

**internal/views/render.go** (18 lines):
- `Render()` function - renders templ.Component to Gin response
- Used by health_handlers.go for templ pages

### **Templates Directory (internal/templates/)**

**14 HTML template files** (using Go html/template):
- admin_performance.html - Performance metrics dashboard
- admin_users.html - User management, roles, activation
- admin_webhooks.html - Webhook endpoint registration
- bookings.html - User's booking list with filters
- bookings_admin.html - All bookings view (staff/admin)
- clinician.html - Clinician patient list by department
- dashboard.html - Legacy health dashboard (now templ-based)
- error.html - Error display page
- layout.html - Base template
- login.html - Legacy login form
- menu.html - Menu display with ordering
- menu_manage.html - Menu management interface
- register.html - User registration form

**funcs.go** - Template functions registration

### **Key Architecture Patterns**

1. **Multi-Tenancy**: organization_id on almost all tables, enforced in handlers
2. **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)**: 5 roles (student, faculty, clinician, staff, admin)
3. **Data Scoping**:
   - Students/Faculty: only their own health/booking records
   - Clinicians: their department's patients
   - Staff: specific departments (based on DepartmentID)
   - Admin: entire organization
4. **Security**:
   - CSRF tokens on form submissions
   - File type validation (magic bytes + declared type)
   - HMAC signed API requests
   - Rate limiting (60/min)
   - Session-based auth with secure cookies
   - Locally-issued API tokens with expiration
5. **Audit Trail**: all changes logged with fingerprinting for compliance
6. **CSV Integration**: automatic user/department sync from CSV files
7. **Webhook System**: internal-only URLs, signed payloads, 3 retries
8. **Reporting**: materialized views refreshed every 15 minutes, cached for 5 minutes
9. **Database Optimization**:
   - Vitals table partitioned by month
   - Connection pooling (25 open, 10 idle)
   - Slow query logging (>500ms)

### **Existing Templ Usage**

- health_handlers.go calls `views.Render(c, http.StatusOK, views.DashboardPage(dd))` for dashboard
- Dashboard demonstrates full templ pattern: data struct → component rendering
- layout.templ provides reusable Layout wrapper with navigation

### **Files Listed**

- **internal/templates/**: 14 HTML files + funcs.go

102				IF NOT is_partitioned THEN

104					CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vitals_backup AS SELECT * FROM vitals;

106					CREATE TABLE vitals (

1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/rand"
5		"encoding/hex"
6		"log"
7		"os"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	type Config struct {
12		DBHost     string
13		DBPort     string
14		DBUser     string
15		DBPassword string
16		DBName     string
17		DBSSLMode  string
18	
19		ServerPort string
20		SessionKey string
21		HMACSecret string
22	
23		UploadDir    string
24		WatchedDir   string
25		MaxUploadMB  int64
26		AllowedTypes []string
27	
28		RateLimitPerMin int
29		TempAccessDur   time.Duration
30		SlowQueryMs     int64
31		CacheTTL        time.Duration
32	
33		MVRefreshInterval time.Duration
34		SecureCookies     bool
35	}
36	
37	func Load() *Config {
38		return &Config{
39			DBHost:     getEnv("DB_HOST", "localhost"),
40			DBPort:     getEnv("DB_PORT", "5432"),
41			DBUser:     getEnv("DB_USER", "campus_admin"),
42			DBPassword: getEnv("DB_PASSWORD", "campus_secret"),
43			DBName:     getEnv("DB_NAME", "campus_portal"),
44			DBSSLMode:  getEnv("DB_SSLMODE", "disable"),
45	
46			ServerPort: getEnv("SERVER_PORT", "8080"),
47			SessionKey: requireEnvOrGenerate("SESSION_KEY"),
48			HMACSecret: requireEnvOrGenerate("HMAC_SECRET"),
49	
50			UploadDir:    getEnv("UPLOAD_DIR", "./uploads"),
51			WatchedDir:   getEnv("WATCHED_DIR", "./watched_folder"),
52			MaxUploadMB:  10,
53			AllowedTypes: []string{"application/pdf", "image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/gif"},
54	
55			RateLimitPerMin:   60,
56			TempAccessDur:     8 * time.Hour,
57			SlowQueryMs:       500,
58			CacheTTL:          5 * time.Minute,
59			MVRefreshInterval: 15 * time.Minute,
60			// Default secure cookies to true in release mode, false in dev
61			SecureCookies: func() bool {
62				explicit := os.Getenv("SECURE_COOKIES")
63				if explicit != "" {
64					return explicit == "true"
65				}
66				return os.Getenv("GIN_MODE") == "release"
67			}(),
68		}
69	}
70	
71	func getEnv(key, fallback string) string {
72		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
73			return v
74		}
75		return fallback
76	}
77	
78	// requireEnvOrGenerate returns the env var value, or generates a random secret
79	// and warns at startup. Production deployments MUST set these via env vars.
80	func requireEnvOrGenerate(key string) string {
81		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
82			return v
83		}
84		b := make([]byte, 32)
85		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
86			log.Fatalf("Failed to generate random secret for %s: %v", key, err)
87		}
88		generated := hex.EncodeToString(b)
89		log.Printf("WARNING: %s not set — generated random value. Set this env var for production.", key)
90		return generated
91	}
